title: Five Football Managers Sacked in Early 2026: A Turbulent Start to the Year
text: The opening days of 2026 brought unprecedented managerial upheaval across British football, with five prominent coaches dismissed within the first week of the year. The wave of dismissals highlighted mounting pressures facing modern football managers. Enzo Maresca - Chelsea (January 1): Chelsea terminated Maresca's contract on New Year's Day following 18 months in charge. Despite earlier accolades, including Conference League and Club World Cup victories alongside Champions League qualification, the Italian's form deteriorated sharply. He won just one of his final seven Premier League matches, prompting the club's decision. Tensions escalated when Maresca cryptically referenced his 'worst 48 hours' at the club following disagreements with medical staff over player workload management. Liam Rosenior, managing Strasbourg, was subsequently appointed. Jimmy Thelin - Aberdeen (January 4): Thelin was sacked after seven months, despite guiding Aberdeen to their first Scottish Cup triumph in 35 years. A five-match winless streak, including four defeats, sealed his fate. The Swedish manager's initial success - winning 13 consecutive matches - gave way to disastrous league form, with the club experiencing their worst campaign start in 26 years. Wilfried Nancy - Celtic (January 5): Nancy holds the unfortunate distinction of becoming Celtic's shortest-serving manager, lasting merely 32 days across eight matches. The Frenchman, arriving from MLS's Columbus Crew, managed only two victories while suffering six defeats. His insistence on implementing a 3-4-3 formation contradicted the interim predecessor's successful seven-of-eight record. Ruben Amorim - Manchester United (January 5): Manchester United removed Amorim after 14 months, citing his worst managerial record in the Premier League era with a 32 percent win ratio. Following an inflammatory press conference demanding greater authority, Amorim departed with just 24 victories from 63 appearances. Ryan Mason - West Bromwich Albion (January 6): Mason's seven-month tenure concluded following a 2-1 defeat to Leicester, extending West Brom's away-match losing streak to a club-record ten consecutive losses. Winning only nine of 27 matches, Mason failed to mount a promotion challenge despite signing a three-year agreement.
